members.name: CorrectPosture
members.signature: public Transform3D CorrectPosture(Transform3D transform, int rotationMode)
members.summary: Correct the [param transform]. [param rotation_mode] implicitly specifies how posture (forward, up and sideway direction) is calculated.

members.name: CubicInterp
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the position between two cached points is interpolated cubically, and linearly otherwise. The points along the [Curve3D] of the [Path3D] are precomputed before use, for faster calculations. The point at the requested offset is then calculated interpolating between two adjacent cached points. This may present a problem if the curve makes sharp turns, as the cached points may not follow the curve closely enough. There are two answers to this problem: either increase the number of cached points and increase memory consumption, or make a cubic interpolation between two points at the cost of (slightly) slower calculations.

members.name: HOffset
members.summary: The node's offset along the curve.

members.name: Loop
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], any offset outside the path's length will wrap around, instead of stopping at the ends. Use it for cyclic paths.

members.name: Progress
members.summary: The distance from the first vertex, measured in 3D units along the path. Changing this value sets this node's position to a point within the path.

members.name: ProgressRatio
members.summary: The distance from the first vertex, considering 0.0 as the first vertex and 1.0 as the last. This is just another way of expressing the progress within the path, as the progress supplied is multiplied internally by the path's length. It can be set or get only if the [PathFollow3D] is the child of a [Path3D] which is part of the scene tree, and that this [Path3D] has a [Curve3D] with a non-zero length. Otherwise, trying to set this field will print an error, and getting this field will return [code]0.0[/code].

members.name: RotationMode
members.summary: Allows or forbids rotation on one or more axes, depending on the [enum RotationMode] constants being used.

members.name: TiltEnabled
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the tilt property of [Curve3D] takes effect.

members.name: UseModelFront
members.summary: If [code]true[/code], the node moves on the travel path with orienting the +Z axis as forward. See also [constant Vector3.FORWARD] and [constant Vector3.MODEL_FRONT].

members.name: VOffset
members.summary: The node's offset perpendicular to the curve.

values.name: ROTATION_ORIENTED
values.summary: Uses the up vector information in a [Curve3D] to enforce orientation. This rotation mode requires the [Path3D]'s [member Curve3D.up_vector_enabled] property to be set to [code]true[/code].

values.name: ROTATION_XY
values.summary: Allows the PathFollow3D to rotate in both the X, and Y axes.

values.name: ROTATION_XYZ
values.summary: Allows the PathFollow3D to rotate in any axis.

values.name: ROTATION_Y
values.summary: Allows the PathFollow3D to rotate in the Y axis only.

constants.name: ROTATION_Y
constants.summary: Allows the PathFollow3D to rotate in the Y axis only.

constants.name: ROTATION_XY
constants.summary: Allows the PathFollow3D to rotate in both the X, and Y axes.

constants.name: ROTATION_XYZ
constants.summary: Allows the PathFollow3D to rotate in any axis.

constants.name: ROTATION_ORIENTED
constants.summary: Uses the up vector information in a [Curve3D] to enforce orientation. This rotation mode requires the [Path3D]'s [member Curve3D.up_vector_enabled] property to be set to [code]true[/code].
